name: AuditLogger_log_event_redacts_pii_and_returns_uuid
description: Security test to ensure AuditLogger.log_event redacts common PII keys (ssn, card, cvv, dob, credit) in the logged payload and returns a UUID-like log id without raising.
input: Instantiate AuditLogger and call log_event(event_type='enrollment_request', user_id='student-123', payload={'name':'Alice','ssn':'123-45-6789','credit_card':'4111-1111-1111-1111','notes':'ok'}) while capturing logger output.
output: A string log_id (UUID format) is returned and the captured log message contains '[REDACTED]' for the PII keys instead of the raw values.
success_criteria: returned value matches UUID format (uuid4 pattern)
no exception is raised by AuditLogger.log_event
captured logger output contains '[REDACTED]' at least once
captured logger output does not contain raw PII values like '123-45-6789' or '4111-1111-1111-1111'
error_scenarios: logging subsystem fails and raises an exception
payload not serializable leading to JSON serialization error (should be handled by default=str)
AuditLogger implementation changes and no longer redacts expected keys
